L1.area: XML parsing, tree traversal, and namespace processing

L2.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the XML namespace processing logic. When the XML parser encounters certain error conditions, it can leak incomplete namespace definitions with null values onto tree nodes. Subsequent operations that enumerate these namespace declarations dereference the null pointers without validation, causing a crash and denial of service.

L3.area: Namespace definition enumeration during iterative tree walking and namespace map construction
L3.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists where namespace definitions with null href values, leaked by the underlying XML parsing library from failed parser runs, are dereferenced without null checks during iterative tree walking operations and namespace map construction, causing a crash and denial of service. This affects usage patterns involving iterwalk and canonicalize functions.

ground_truth.reason: The iterwalk and namespace map construction functions dereference c_ns.href without null checks, causing a crash when libxml2 leaks empty namespace definitions from failed parser runs.
